items.title: Djibouti president wins election with 97 8% of vote, state media says
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>President Ismael Omar Guelleh has won re-election with 97.8% of the vote, securing a sixth term and extending his 27-year rule in Djibouti, state media reports.

#News #Reuters #Newsfeed #africa #djibouti #election #politics #world #worldnews

items.title: Trump orders Hormuz blockade after failed US Iran peace talks
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>President Trump said the US Navy would blockade the Strait of Hormuz and interdict every vessel in international waters that paid a toll to Iran after peace talks failed.

items.title: Hormuz blockade, Supreme Court retirements and powering AI with coal
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Negotiators leave Islamabad without a deal to end the U.S.–Iran war, putting a fragile ceasefire and global energy flows at risk. Trump says the U.S. will blockade the Strait of Hormuz. Russia and Ukraine trade accusations of Orthodox Easter ceasefire violations. How looming U.S. midterms could reshape the Supreme Court, and why Washington is turning back to coal to power the AI revolution.

#News #Reuters #Newsfeed

items.title: 📺 Elle Fanning's ‘Margo’s Got Money Troubles’ explores OnlyFans
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>The worlds of sex work, motherhood and family are combined in the new limited series "Margo's Got Money Troubles" created by actors, sisters, and producing partners Elle and Dakota Fanning, based on Rufi Thorpe's bestselling novel with the same name. "The series is a lot about judgment and breaking down the walls of a preconceived notion of someone or of why they are doing something," Elle Fanning said.

#onlyfans #ellefanning #dakotafanning #rufithorpe #tvseries</article>

items.content:

items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Hungary votes today, in what could be the country’s most consequential election in years. This week’s bank earnings kick off with expectations still high, but investors will be listening closely for how Wall Street talks about geopolitical risk and private credit.

items.author: Reuters
items.content:
